titlestmt.titleproper: Guide to the Harold J. Mayers Papers
titlestmt.author: Preliminary Inventory by Joe Anderson

conventiondeclaration.abbr: Finding aid created in accordance with Manuscripts and Archives Processing Manual
conventiondeclaration.citation: translation missing: en.enumerations.resource_finding_aid_description_rules.Finding aid created in accordance with Manuscripts and Archives Processing Manual

maintenanceevent.eventdescription: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on Thursday August 20, 2020 at 08:11

did.abstract: The papers consist of correspondence, administrative files, subject files, printed materials and reports of Harold J. Mayers. The papers also contain files from six national and Washington, D.C., public health and tuberculosis organizations, including the American Public Health Association and National Tuberculosis Association. These papers form part of the Contemporary Medical Care and Health Policy Collection.

prefercite.head: Preferred Citation
prefercite.p: Harold J. Mayers Papers (MS 938). Manuscripts and Archives, Yale University Library.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Arranged in two series: I. UNITED MINE WORKERS OF AMERICA, WELFARE AND RETIREMENT FUND PAPERS. II. ORGANIZATIONS.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: 4 paige boxes; received from Harold J. Mayers

The papers consist of correspondence, administrative files, subject files, printed materials and reports of Harold J. Mayers. The papers also contain files from six national and Washington, D.C., public health and tuberculosis organizations, including the American Public Health Association and National Tuberculosis Association. These papers form part of the Contemporary Medical Care and Health Policy Collection.

bioghist.head: Biographical / Historical
bioghist.p: Harold J. Mayers was a hospital consultant with the United Mine Workers of America Welfare and Retirement Fund from 1946 to 1976. The bulk of the papers consists of the correspondence files and administrative records from Mayers' tenure at the Fund. The early papers are fragmentary; the papers from 1973 to 1975 are more complete. In addition, the collection contains Mayers' files from five national and local (Washington, D.C.) public health and tuberculosis organizations.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: The materials are open for research.

userestrict.head: Conditions Governing Use
userestrict.p: Copyright has been transferred to Yale University for unpublished materials authored or otherwise produced by the creator(s) of this collection. Copyright status for other collection materials is unknown. Transmission or reproduction of materials protected by U.S. Copyright Law (Title 17, U.S.C.) beyond that allowed by fair use requires the written permission of the copyright owners. Works not in the public domain cannot be commercially exploited without permission of the copyright owners. Responsibility for any use rests exclusively with the user.

title.part: Contemporary Medical Care and Health Policy Collection

corpname.part: United Mine Workers of America. Welfare and Retirement Fund

did.unittitle: UNITED MINE WORKERS OF AMERICA, WELFARE AND RETIREMENT FUND PAPERS
Clinic Health Care Plan, Inception: memoranda, reports
Clinic Health Care Plan, Medical Management: memoranda, reports
Conference on Technology and Health Care Systems: memoranda, clippings
Faber, Joan K., "A Thousand Bedsides," drafts
Memphis Health Center, Inc.: notes, correspondence
Migratory Labor Health Association Hospital: correspondence, policy statements, operations manual
Physician Extenders, Massachusetts Institute of Technology: correspondence
Quality Assurance in Hospital Care Meeting: correspondence
Transit Employees' Health and Welfare Fund: memoranda, print material
Annual reports, Welfare and Retirement Fund
Memos re: hospital service to Fund beneficiaries as a percent of total hospital service
"An analysis of the mental health services of the United Mine Workers of America Welfare and Retirement Fund"; related correspondence and print material
Reprints re: Welfare and Retirement Fund
John L. Lewis: transcripts of congressional testimony
Weinerman, E. Richard, "Report of a Survey of the Miners Memorial Association 1960-1961"
Subcommittee on Social Action: annual report
Committee for the Nation's Health: newsletters
District of Columbia Public Health Association
National Tuberculosis and Respiratory Disease Association, Committee on Metropolitan Problems
National Tuberculosis Association, Christmas Seal Campaign Committee: correspondence, memoranda
National Tuberculosis Association, Committee on Treatment of Tuberculosis Patients in Hospitals: reports, correspondence
